assembly_instructions: Compact open-source emergency ventilator in a ≈20×20×20 cm enclosure. The base casing carries two stage plates — a pneumatics stage (blower, piping, filters, valves) on one half and an electronics stage (control boards, plugs, cooling) on the other. Air flows from the exterior air plug through the blower-input filter into the blower, then through a connector pipe into an oxygen mixer, past a pressure valve, through a patient filter, and out to the patient. An exhaust filter handles return air. A fan holder at the rear keeps the electronics bay cool.

info.japanese_name: MakAir オープンソース人工呼吸器
info.summary: Emergency-grade, open-hardware ventilator designed by the Makers-For-Life collective during the COVID-19 pandemic. Targeted at hospital shortages in low-resource settings.
info.description: MakAir is an open-source emergency ventilator designed in spring 2020 by Makers For Life, a volunteer collective of French engineers, medical professionals, and makers responding to the COVID-19 ventilator shortage. Unlike the one-off 'motor-compresses-an-AMBU-bag' prototypes that circulated early in the pandemic, MakAir is a compact, self-contained blower-based ventilator — a brushless blower draws ambient air through a HEPA filter, mixes it with optional supplemental oxygen in a passive mixer, meters it past a proportional pressure valve, and delivers it through a disposable patient filter to the patient circuit. A separate exhaust-return filter handles expired gas. All pneumatics live on one stage plate, all electronics (STM32 controller, motor driver, sensors, UI) on another. The enclosure is a ≈20 × 20 × 20 cm cube with a front touchscreen; CAD, firmware, and bill of materials are released under open licenses so hospitals can manufacture locally.
info.operator: Field-deployed by Makers For Life partners and hospital maker-programs in 2020–21
info.contractor: Makers For Life (volunteer French engineering/medical collective)
info.contract_value: Target ≈€500 in parts (self-build) vs. €20k+ for commercial ICU ventilators
info.status: Open-source design; reference device certified for emergency use in several jurisdictions during COVID-19

facts.label: Architecture
facts.value: Brushless blower + passive O₂ mixer + pressure-valve metering

facts.label: Filters
facts.value: HEPA on blower intake, patient circuit, and exhaust return

facts.label: Oxygen
facts.value: Optional supplemental O₂ via the integrated mixer

facts.label: Alarms
facts.value: Pressure, flow, O₂, disconnection (required for emergency-use certification)

sources.title: Makers For Life — MakAir on GitHub

parts.name: Casing (Base Plate)
parts.role: Outer enclosure floor — every internal stage bolts onto it. Side walls and top cover are removed here for inspectability.

parts.name: Pneumatics Stage
parts.role: Raised floor plate on the pneumatics half — carries the blower, piping, filters, and valves.

parts.name: Electronics Stage
parts.role: Raised floor plate on the electronics half — carries the controller, motor driver, plugs, and cooling fan.

parts.name: Blower Holder
parts.role: Printed cradle that holds the brushless blower module and couples its output to the piping manifold.

parts.name: Piping Holder
parts.role: Cylindrical clamp that routes the flexible air hoses between the blower, mixer, and valves.

parts.name: Blower → O₂-Mixer Connector
parts.role: Machined connector linking the blower output pipe to the O₂ mixer input.

parts.name: Filter Box (Blower Intake)
parts.role: HEPA filter on the blower-intake line — protects the blower and patient from room contaminants.

parts.name: Filter Box Top Fit
parts.role: Snap-on top cap that holds the HEPA element in the blower-intake filter box.

parts.name: Oxygen Mixer
parts.role: Passive mixing chamber that blends room air from the blower with optional supplemental O₂.

parts.name: Pressure Valve
parts.role: Proportional pressure-control valve that meters the breath waveform into the patient circuit.

parts.name: Patient Filter Box
parts.role: Disposable HEPA filter on the patient side — swapped between patients for cross-contamination control.

parts.name: Filter Box (Exhaust Out)
parts.role: HEPA filter on the exhaust-return line — scrubs expired gas before it vents out of the device.

parts.name: Fan Holder
parts.role: Printed mount for the electronics-bay cooling fan.

parts.name: Air Plug (external port)
parts.role: Pneumatic inlet/outlet quick-connect on the enclosure side wall.

parts.name: Electrical Plugs
parts.role: Power inlet + service/data connectors on the rear of the electronics stage.
